questions.question: A noisy image is represented in the frequency domain as a sum of the original signal and a periodic noise component. If the noise has a frequency separation of 2π times the Nyquist frequency, what type of filtering is most effective in removing the noise?

Consider an image degraded by a low-pass filter with a cutoff frequency of 10 Hz. The image is then filtered using a band-pass filter with a central frequency of 5 Hz. What is the effect of this filtering on the high-frequency components of the image?

A periodic noise component in an image can be represented as a periodic function in the frequency domain. If the noise period is T, what is the minimum sampling rate required to accurately represent the noise component?
